# Course Overview {.unnumbered}

Practical, short course on core statistical tools and interpreting results for clinical research. Focus: data summary, uncertainty, visualization, and clear communication.

## What You Will Learn {.unnumbered}

- Summarize and visualize data clearly.
- Quantify uncertainty with confidence intervals and tests.
- Fit and interpret basic regression and classification models.
- Report findings in plain language and produce reproducible analyses.

## Methods covered in this course {.unnumbered}

- Descriptive statistics and data transformation
- Confidence intervals, hypothesis tests, t-tests, ANOVA
- Regression (linear, logistic) and model diagnostics
- Survival analysis and time-to-event methods
- Effective data visualization

## Why This Course {.unnumbered}

Helps clinicians and researchers interpret numerical results, decide when findings matter, and communicate conclusions clearly using reproducible R workflows.

# Aims {#Aims}

1. Read and interpret statistical summaries and plots.
2. Communicate uncertainty and clinical relevance.
3. Build reproducible analysis reports.

# Learning Objectives {#Learning-Objectives}

After this course you will be able to:

1. Summarize and visualize data to answer research questions.
2. Quantify uncertainty and apply appropriate tests.
3. Fit basic regression models and interpret coefficients.
4. Produce clear, reproducible reports that explain results.

# Prerequisites {#Prerequisites}

- Basic familiarity with statistics.
- Ability to install R packages; RStudio recommended.

Follow the setup instructions in the course materials before starting the exercises.
